title: Poo Pookkum Osai Song Lyrics Minsara Kanavu
stanzas:
Poo Pookkum Osai Song Lyrics Minsara Kanavu
#ARRahman #Sujatha #Vairamuthu

hmm patchigalin
koo koo koo
poochigalin
ring ring ring
sangeetham sollitharume
thangap penne

kaalodu salangai pootti
tharai ellaam veenai meetti
radhi paadum paadal
kaelaai pattup penne

kaetkum oli ellaam
ada sari gama padha
nisa ree.....

kan kaanaa thoorathil
suthi serum thaalathil
rail pogum oasai
sangeetham.

karai konda paaraimel
kadal konda alai vandhu
kaithattum oasai
sangeetham.

hmm patchigalin
koo koo koo
poochigalin
ring ring ring
sangeetham sollitharume
thangap penne

kaalodu salangai pootti
tharai ellaam veenai meetti

kaelaai pattup penne

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

Poo Pookkum Osai conveys the beauty of love and the longing associated with it, using nature as a metaphor.

What is the context of this song in the movie?

In Minsara Kanavu, this song illustrates the romantic feelings between the lead characters, enhancing the emotional depth of their relationship.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the talented Sujatha Mohan, known for her soulful voice and emotive singing style.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Poo Pookkum Osai was composed by the legendary AR Rahman, marking one of his iconic works in Tamil cinema.

When was this song released?

Poo Pookkum Osai was released on January 14, 1997, as part of the film Minsara Kanavu.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Poo Pookkum Osai, the enchanting melody unfolds as a celebration of love, perfectly encapsulating the emotions of the characters in Minsara Kanavu. The song's soothing rhythm and poetic lyrics evoke a sense of nostalgia and longing. It beautifully portrays the connection between nature and love, making it a timeless classic. With Sujatha Mohan's captivating voice and AR Rahman's exquisite composition, this song remains a cherished favorite among fans.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Poo Pookkum Osai from Minsara Kanavu is a mesmerizing melody that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. With Sujatha Mohan's enchanting vocals and AR Rahman's magical composition, this song transports listeners to a dreamy realm. Experience the lyrical brilliance of Vairamuthu as it weaves a tale of romance that resonates deeply with the heart.
stanzaMeanings: The sound of flowers blooming is a desire to listen to.
The sound of grass growing is a desire to listen to.
Hmm, the sounds of the green fields and the ringing of flowers create music, dear.
With the anklet on my feet, I play the veena on the stage, singing songs that you will hear, dear.
The earth is like a veena, played by the hands of the wind.
All the sounds that you hear are in perfect harmony...
The sound of the blooming flowers is a desire to listen to.
The sound of grass growing is a desire to listen to.
In the time when the eyes are closed, there is a sound of silence that sings.
The sound of the train passing by in the distance is music.
At the time of hunger, there is a sound of joy... (2) joyful music...
The music that plays when all the brothers and sisters bless their children.
The sound of the blooming flowers is a desire to listen to.
The sound of grass growing is a desire to listen to.
The chirping of small birds and the fluttering of wings create music.
The waves of the sea crashing on the shore create music.
The sound of the wind calling for honey (2) is sweet music.
The sound of raindrops falling on the ground creates music.
The sound of the blooming flowers is a desire to listen to.
The sound of grass growing is a desire to listen to.
Hmm, the sounds of the green fields and the ringing of flowers create music, dear.
With the anklet on my feet, I play the veena on the stage, singing songs that you will hear, dear.
The earth is like a veena, played by the hands of the wind.
All the sounds that you hear are in perfect harmony...
